ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cancer_sites" (
  "cancer_site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cancer site */,
  "cancer_site_name" TEXT /* Name of the cancer site (e.g., Breast, Lung) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cancer site */,
  "common_in_age_group" TEXT /* Age group commonly affected by the cancer site */,
  "prevalence_rate" REAL /* Prevalence rate of the cancer site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cancer_site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state (e.g., Alabama, California) */,
  "state_code" INTEGER /* Code for the state (e.g., 1, 5) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the state */,
  "geographic_area" TEXT /* Geographic area of the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "race_name" TEXT /* Name of the race (e.g., Black or African American, White) */,
  "race_code" TEXT /* Code for the race (e.g., 2054-5, 2106-3) */,
  "percentage_of_population" REAL /* Percentage of the state's population */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sexes" (
  "sex_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sex */,
  "sex_name" TEXT /* Name of the sex (e.g., Female, Male) */,
  "sex_code" TEXT /* Code for the sex (e.g., F, M) */,
  "percentage_of_population" REAL /* Percentage of the state's population */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sex_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year_name" INTEGER /* Year for which cancer data is collected (e.g., 2000, 2001) */,
  "year_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the year */,
  "year_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cancer_data" (
  "cancer_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cancer data record */,
  "cancer_site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cancer site */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* ID of the racial demographic */,
  "sex_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sex demographic */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths due to cancer */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population size */,
  "age_adjusted_rate" REAL /* Age-adjusted rate of cancer */,
  "incidence_rate" REAL /* Incidence rate of cancer */,
  "survival_rate" REAL /* Survival rate of cancer */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., CDC, WHO) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cancer_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cancer_data_race_id FOREIGN KEY ("race_id") REFERENCES races ("race_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cancer_data_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cancer_data_cancer_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("cancer_site_id") REFERENCES cancer_sites ("cancer_site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cancer_data_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cancer_data_sex_id FOREIGN KEY ("sex_id") REFERENCES sexes ("sex_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team of the user */,
  "security_level" TEXT /* Security level of the user (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "cancer_data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cancer data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_cancer_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("cancer_data_id") REFERENCES cancer_data ("cancer_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
